subject: [PATCH man-pages v2 0/6] userfaultfd: document read-write-protect mode

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch description does not mention the changes made to the HISTORY section and requested clarification on what was changed.

Reviewer noted that the patch description should not include the 'Addresses Alejandro Colomar's review' section, as it is redundant and should be removed.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 2026-05-26T14:41:44+0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Thanks!  I've applied the patch.


Have a lovely night!
Alex

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch description does not include a link to the matching kernel series and requested that it be added

Reviewer noted that the patch description should include the kernel series version number, as it is not specified in the commit message.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 2026-05-26T14:41:45+0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Thanks!  I've applied the patch (with a few minor tweaks).


Have a lovely day!
Alex

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch description does not mention the changes made to the kernel series, and requested that it be updated to reflect this.

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch series documents the userfaultfd read-write-protect mode, a working-set tracking mechanism for VM guest memory introduced in Linux 7.2. The patches update various man pages to include new documentation on UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP and related features, including UFFD_FEATURE_RWP and UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC. The changes address review comments and ensure consistency with existing documentation.
